postmanEvidence: newman@6.2.2 differential evidence covers Digest challenge retry, Hawk, and AWS Signature v4 behavior through pm.sendRequest.
Installed Postman Desktop runtime/source audit classifies proxy, TLS, configured client-certificate, and unsupported advanced-auth boundaries.

observations: PostMeter now applies supported advanced auth helpers in the parent-owned HTTP broker, never in the script worker.
Scripts can request configured client-certificate bindings by ID, but script-provided certificate/key/PFX paths fail closed before the parent reads any path.
Proxy options are normalized into a parent-owned transport configuration. Scripts never receive raw sockets, agents, CONNECT tunnel handles, or file handles.
TLS certificate validation remains strict for pm.sendRequest. Script-level strictSSL=false and equivalent rejectUnauthorized=false forms fail closed.

NTLM and Akamai EdgeGrid helper shapes are preserved/imported/exported and produce explicit unsupported errors in the broker until exact Postman-compatible stateful handshake/signing implementations are added; they remain explicit parity blocker rows.
